news.headline: uniQure CEO Kapusta exercises options and sells 21,082 shares under 10b5-1 plan
news.summary: CEO Matthew Kapusta exercised options on 21,082 shares at $19.39 and sold the same number of shares in the $44-$46 range under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. After the transactions he directly owned 519,227 ordinary shares.

news.headline: uniQure closes upsized $259 million public offering after underwriters fully exercise option
news.summary: uniQure closed its upsized share offering, selling 5,686,813 ordinary shares at $45.50 including full exercise of the underwriters' over-allotment option, for gross proceeds of about $259 million. Proceeds will fund commercialization readiness for AMT-130, potential commercial launch activities, and the planned confirmatory study.

news.headline: uniQure prices upsized $225 million public offering of ordinary shares
news.summary: uniQure priced an upsized underwritten public offering of 4,945,055 ordinary shares at $45.50 per share, targeting roughly $225 million in gross proceeds, capitalizing on the stock's post-FDA-update rally to strengthen its balance sheet.

news.headline: uniQure reports encouraging preliminary AMT-260 data in refractory epilepsy
news.summary: In the first, low-dose cohort of six patients in the Phase I/IIa trial of AMT-260 for refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy, three patients had 79%-100% reductions in disabling seizures at months 4-6, with a generally well-tolerated safety profile and no therapy-related serious adverse events. A second, higher-dose cohort is enrolling, with combined results expected in H1 2027.

news.headline: Wall Street sharply raises QURE price targets after FDA reversal on AMT-130
news.source: Investing.com (Mizuho, RBC Capital, Cantor Fitzgerald ratings)
news.summary: Following the FDA's regulatory U-turn, Mizuho raised its target to $68 from $35, RBC Capital raised its target to $65 from $35, and Cantor Fitzgerald upgraded uniQure to Overweight from Neutral with a target of $61, up from $18, citing improved visibility on accelerated approval.

news.headline: FDA reverses course, clears path for uniQure to file AMT-130 BLA in Q3 2026
news.summary: The FDA agreed that uniQure's 3-year Phase I/II data can serve as the primary basis for an accelerated-approval BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, reversing its November 2025 objection to using the Enroll-HD external control; uniQure said it will submit the U.S. BLA and a UK MHRA marketing application in Q3 2026. Shares surged as much as ~75-80% intraday on the news.

catalysts.label: US BLA submission for AMT-130 (accelerated approval)
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to submit a Biologics License Application to the FDA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease based on 3-year Phase I/II data, after the FDA agreed this data can serve as the primary basis for accelerated approval.

catalysts.label: UK MHRA Marketing Authorisation Application (MAA) submission
catalysts.detail: Following a successful pre-submission meeting, uniQure plans to file a MAA with the UK's MHRA for AMT-130 based on the same 3-year dataset, seeking a parallel approval path in the UK.

catalysts.label: Confirmatory study initiation for AMT-130
catalysts.detail: uniQure and the FDA are working to finalize a confirmatory study design using a concurrent standard-of-care control (rather than a sham-surgery control); the company has committed to starting this study without delay once design is finalized.

catalysts.label: Updated AMT-260 data (low- and high-dose cohorts) in refractory epilepsy
catalysts.detail: uniQure expects to present combined follow-up results from both the low-dose and higher-dose (3x10^12 gc/mL) cohorts of its Phase I/IIa AMT-260 trial in mesial temporal lobe epilepsy.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: uniQure has not yet announced an exact date, but based on prior-quarter timing is expected to report Q2 2026 financial results and pipeline updates in late July or early August 2026, including cash runway updates following its June capital raise.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA decision on AMT-130 accelerated approval
catalysts.detail: If the Q3 2026 BLA is accepted for priority/accelerated review, an FDA approval decision could follow roughly 6-10 months later; no formal PDUFA date exists yet since the BLA has not been submitted.

pulse: uniQure has been transformed over the past two months: on 2026-06-17 the FDA reversed its earlier objection and agreed that uniQure's 3-year Phase I/II data can support an accelerated-approval BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, sending shares up roughly 75-80% in a single session and triggering a wave of analyst upgrades. The company is now moving to submit both a US BLA and a UK MHRA MAA in Q3 2026, and it raised an upsized $259 million public offering in late June to fund commercial-launch readiness and a still-to-be-designed confirmatory study, extending its cash runway into the second half of 2029. Beyond the flagship Huntington's program, early pipeline signals are also encouraging, with the first AMT-260 gene therapy cohort in refractory epilepsy showing meaningful seizure reductions in half of treated patients.
